title: Fix blog post display, add inline image upload, and restore green theme

requirements.summary: Debug and fix why uploaded blog posts are not displaying on the blog listing page and ensure all published posts appear correctly
acceptanceCriteria: All uploaded blog posts with published status are visible on the blog listing page
Blog posts display with correct title, excerpt, image, and metadata
Posts are accessible via their individual detail pages

file_operations.description: Review and debug the published posts fetching and rendering logic. Verify the useGetPublishedPosts hook is correctly filtering and displaying posts. Add error boundary handling and detailed logging to identify why posts may not be appearing. Ensure the PostCard component receives all required props correctly.

Review the useGetPublishedPosts query implementation to ensure it correctly calls the backend getPublishedPosts method and handles the response. Add error handling and verify the query key and cache invalidation logic are correct.

Verify the PostCard component correctly handles all blog post data including image rendering using ExternalBlob.getDirectURL(). Ensure proper fallback handling for missing images and that all metadata displays correctly.

Verify individual post detail pages correctly fetch and display full post data by slug. Ensure the post content, images, and metadata render properly and that navigation from the listing page works correctly.

requirements.summary: Add image upload functionality within the blog post rich text editor to allow inserting images in the middle of blog post content
acceptanceCriteria: Rich text editor (ReactQuill) includes an image upload button in the toolbar
Users can upload images and insert them at any position within the blog post content
Uploaded images are stored properly and display correctly in both editor and published post view
Image uploads integrate with existing blob storage system

file_operations.description: Extend the ReactQuill editor configuration to add a custom image upload handler in the toolbar. Implement an image handler that opens a file picker, converts the selected file to ExternalBlob using ExternalBlob.fromBytes(), and inserts the image URL into the editor at the current cursor position using getDirectURL(). Track uploaded content images in the contentImages state array and pass them to the createPost mutation. Verify the blob-storage component's usage instructions before implementing. Add upload progress indicators using withUploadProgress().

Apply the same ReactQuill image upload handler implementation as in CreateBlogPostPage. Ensure existing contentImages from the post are preserved and new uploads are appended to the array. Handle both new and existing ExternalBlob instances correctly when updating the post.
Ensure the post detail page correctly renders inline images from the content HTML. Verify that image URLs generated by getDirectURL() are accessible and display properly in the published post view.

requirements.summary: Restore the website color scheme to green theme as it appeared in earlier versions, replacing the current blue-green palette
acceptanceCriteria: Primary color palette uses green tones instead of blue-green
All UI components (buttons, cards, badges, links) reflect the green theme
Both light and dark mode variants use appropriate green color values
Theme colors are consistently applied across all pages

file_operations.description: Replace the current blue-green OKLCH color variables with green theme colors. Update --primary, --primary-foreground, --accent, --accent-foreground, and related color variables to use green hues (approximately 142-150 degrees in OKLCH) instead of the current blue-green values. Adjust both light and dark mode variants to maintain proper contrast and accessibility. Update any custom utility classes that reference theme colors.

Update the Tailwind color configuration to align with the new green theme. Modify any HSL-based color variables or custom color definitions to use green tones. Ensure the theme colors referenced throughout the codebase (bg-primary, text-primary, border-primary, etc.) correctly map to the new green palette.
